div1.entryFree:
Lot, 1) a thing used in determining chances: if we draw --s, Ant. II, 3, 35. II, 6, 62. II, 6, 62 Per. I, 4, 46. as by l. Hml. II, 2, 435. it is --s to blanks == it is very probable, Cor. V, 2, 10.
2) fate: bequeath not to their l. the shame, Lucr. 534. however God or fortune cast my l. R2 I, 3, 85.
3) a tax ('pars tributi sive solutionis alicujus, quam inter alios quis tenetur praestare.' Spelman): that hot termagant Scot had paid me scot and l. too, H4A V, 4, 115.
